description: Search for or read a FHIR AdverseEvent resource tracking harmful or potentially harmful healthcare events. For direct read, provide _id alone. For search, provide subject (patient reference), study, resultingcondition, or substance. Note: R4 uses 'subject' not 'patient' for this resource. Use actuality to distinguish actual vs. potential events.

searchParams._id: AdverseEvent resource ID — performs direct read when provided alone
searchParams.actuality: Whether event is actual or potential
searchParams.study: Research study associated with the event
searchParams.subject: Subject (patient) impacted by the event

description: Search for or read a FHIR AllergyIntolerance resource. For direct read, provide _id alone. For search, provide patient or identifier. Use category to filter by food, medication, environment, or biologic. Use clinical-status to filter active, inactive, or resolved allergies.

searchParams._id: AllergyIntolerance resource ID — performs direct read when provided alone
searchParams.asserter: Source of the information (Patient, RelatedPerson, Practitioner)
searchParams.category: Allergy category: food, medication, environment, biologic
searchParams.last-date: Date of last known occurrence of a reaction
searchParams.manifestation: Clinical symptoms/signs associated with the event
searchParams.onset: Date of onset of the allergy or intolerance

description: Search for or read a FHIR Appointment resource for scheduled healthcare events. For direct read, provide _id alone. For search, provide patient, identifier, actor, practitioner, location, slot, or based-on. Use date for time filtering and status to find booked, arrived, or cancelled appointments.

searchParams._id: Appointment resource ID — performs direct read when provided alone
searchParams.actor: Any individual participating (patient, practitioner, device, location)
searchParams.based-on: Service request (order) that led to this appointment
searchParams.part-status: Participation status of a participant (accepted, declined, tentative, needs-action)
searchParams.reason-code: Coded reason the appointment is scheduled
searchParams.reason-reference: Condition, Procedure, Observation, or ImmunizationRecommendation reason
searchParams.slot: Slot that this appointment is filling
searchParams.status: Status: proposed, pending, booked, arrived, fulfilled, cancelled, noshow, entered-in-error, checked-in, waitlist
searchParams.supporting-info: Additional information to support the appointment

description: Search for or read a FHIR CarePlan resource describing intended care for a patient. For direct read, provide _id alone. For search, provide patient, subject, identifier, encounter, condition, care-team, or based-on. Use category to distinguish plan types and status to filter active, completed, or draft plans.

searchParams._id: CarePlan resource ID — performs direct read when provided alone
searchParams.activity-reference: Activity details defined in specific resource (Reference)
searchParams.care-team: CareTeam reference involved in the plan
searchParams.condition: Health issues (Condition references) the plan addresses
searchParams.encounter: Encounter associated with the care plan
searchParams.performer: Matches if the practitioner is listed as a performer in any activity
searchParams.status: Status: draft, active, on-hold, revoked, completed, entered-in-error, unknown
searchParams.subject: Subject of the care plan (Patient or Group)

description: Search for or read a FHIR CareTeam resource listing people and organizations involved in a patient's care. For direct read, provide _id alone. For search, provide patient, subject, identifier, encounter, or participant. Use status to filter active, inactive, or proposed teams.

searchParams._id: CareTeam resource ID — performs direct read when provided alone
searchParams.patient: Patient the care team is for
searchParams.status: Status: proposed, active, suspended, inactive, entered-in-error
searchParams.subject: Subject of the care team (Patient or Group)

description: Search for or read a FHIR Condition resource covering both problem list items and encounter diagnoses. For direct read, provide _id alone. For search, provide patient or encounter (at least one required). Use category=problem-list-item for the patient's problem list (clinical-status: active, resolved, inactive) or category=encounter-diagnosis for diagnoses documented during encounters. Omitting category returns both types.

searchParams._id: Condition resource ID — performs direct read when provided alone
searchParams.patient: Patient resource ID or reference — required unless encounter is provided
searchParams.encounter: Encounter resource ID — use instead of patient to get diagnoses for a specific encounter
searchParams.category: problem-list-item for problem list entries, encounter-diagnosis for encounter diagnoses; omit for both
searchParams.clinical-status: Clinical status: active, resolved, or inactive

description: Search for or read a FHIR DiagnosticReport resource including lab results, pathology, and imaging reports. For direct read, provide _id alone. For search, provide patient, subject, identifier, encounter, or based-on. Use category to filter by discipline (e.g. LAB, RAD).

searchParams._id: DiagnosticReport resource ID — performs direct read when provided alone
searchParams.based-on: Reference to the service request (order) that triggered this report
searchParams.date: Clinically relevant date or date range
searchParams.media: Reference to media (images) included in the report
searchParams.performer: Who is responsible for the report
searchParams.status: Report status: registered, partial, preliminary, final, amended, corrected, appended, cancelled, entered-in-error, unknown
searchParams.subject: Subject of the report (Patient or Group)

description: Search for or read a FHIR DocumentReference resource indexing clinical documents, notes, PDFs, and images. For direct read, provide _id alone. For search, provide patient, subject, identifier, encounter, author, custodian, or relatesto. Use category or type to narrow results.

searchParams._id: DocumentReference resource ID — performs direct read when provided alone
searchParams.author: Who authored the document
searchParams.contenttype: MIME type of the document content
searchParams.date: When the document reference was created
searchParams.facility: Kind of facility where patient was seen
searchParams.location: URI where the data can be found
searchParams.period: Time period the document content covers
searchParams.relation: Type of relationship: replaces, transforms, signs, appends
searchParams.setting: Additional context of the document content (practice setting)
searchParams.subject: Subject of the document (Patient or Group)

description: Search for or read a FHIR Encounter resource. Provide an _id for direct read, or search by patient, status, class, or date.

searchParams._id: Encounter resource ID — performs direct read when provided alone
searchParams.date: Encounter date or date range (e.g. ge2024-01-01)

description: Search for or read a FHIR Immunization resource covering vaccine administration history. For direct read, provide _id alone. For search, provide patient or identifier. Use vaccine-code for specific vaccines or date for time-based filtering.

searchParams._id: Immunization resource ID — performs direct read when provided alone
searchParams.location: Location where the vaccination was administered
searchParams.performer: Practitioner or organization who performed the vaccination
searchParams.reaction-date: Date of reaction to the immunization
searchParams.reason-reference: Condition or Observation why vaccine was given
searchParams.target-disease: Target disease the vaccine protects against

description: Search for or read a FHIR List resource — a curated collection of resources such as medication lists, allergy lists, or problem lists. For direct read, provide _id alone. For search, provide patient, subject, identifier, encounter, item, or source. Use code to identify list purpose.

searchParams._id: List resource ID — performs direct read when provided alone
searchParams.code: Purpose of the list (e.g. allergy list, medication list)
searchParams.item: Reference to an actual entry in the list
searchParams.subject: Subject of the list (Patient or Group)
searchParams.title: Descriptive name of the list

description: Search for or read a FHIR Location resource for physical places where services are provided. For direct read, provide _id alone. For search, provide identifier, name, organization, partof, address, address-city, or near. Use near with format latitude|longitude|distance|units (WGS84 datum, defaults to km) for proximity searches.

searchParams._id: Location resource ID — performs direct read when provided alone
searchParams.address-use: Use of the address (work, temp, old)
searchParams.endpoint: Technical endpoints providing access to services at this location
searchParams.name: A portion of the location's name or alias
searchParams.near: Search by proximity — format: latitude|longitude|distance|units (WGS84 datum, units default to km)
searchParams.operational-status: Operational status of the location (typically for bed/room)
searchParams.organization: Organization responsible for provisioning and upkeep
searchParams.type: Type of function performed at the location

description: Search for or read a FHIR Medication resource (drug catalog). For direct read, provide _id alone. For search, provide code, identifier, or another scoping param. This resource describes medication products — use MedicationRequest for prescriptions or MedicationStatement for reported usage.

searchParams._id: Medication resource ID — performs direct read when provided alone
searchParams.code: Medication code (e.g. RxNorm, SNOMED CT, NDC)
searchParams.expiration-date: Expiration date of a medication batch
searchParams.ingredient: Ingredient reference (Reference to Substance or Medication)

description: Search for or read a FHIR MedicationRequest resource (prescriptions and medication orders). For direct read, provide _id alone. For search, provide patient, subject, identifier, encounter, or requester. Filter by status (active, completed, stopped) or intent (order, plan, proposal).

searchParams._id: MedicationRequest resource ID — performs direct read when provided alone
searchParams.category: Medication usage category (e.g. inpatient, outpatient, community)
searchParams.date: Date or date range for the prescription activity
searchParams.encounter: Encounter during which the request was created
searchParams.intent: Intent: proposal, plan, order, original-order, reflex-order, filler-order, instance-order, option
searchParams.medication: Medication reference (when medication is a Reference)
searchParams.status: Status: active, on-hold, cancelled, completed, entered-in-error, stopped, draft, unknown
searchParams.subject: Subject of the request (Patient or Group)

description: Search for or read a FHIR MedicationStatement resource recording medications a patient is taking, has taken, or may take. For direct read, provide _id alone. For search, provide patient, subject, identifier, or context. Use status to filter active, completed, intended, stopped, or on-hold statements.

searchParams._id: MedicationStatement resource ID — performs direct read when provided alone
searchParams.category: Medication usage category (e.g. inpatient, outpatient, community, patientspecified)
searchParams.context: Encounter or EpisodeOfCare associated with the statement
searchParams.effective: Date when patient was taking (or not taking) the medication
searchParams.medication: Medication reference (when medication is a Reference)
searchParams.part-of: Part of referenced event (MedicationAdministration, MedicationDispense, etc.)
searchParams.source: Who or where the information came from (Patient, Practitioner, RelatedPerson, Organization)
searchParams.status: Status: active, completed, entered-in-error, intended, stopped, on-hold, unknown, not-taken
searchParams.subject: Subject of the statement (Patient or Group)

description: Search for or read a FHIR Observation resource. For direct read, provide _id alone. For search, patient is always required plus at least one of category or code. Common categories: laboratory, vital-signs, core-characteristics. Use category=core-characteristics with code 76516-4 (gestational age) or 8339-4 (birth weight) for core characteristic lookups.

searchParams._id: Observation resource ID — performs direct read when provided alone
searchParams.patient: Patient resource ID or reference — required for search

description: Search for or read a FHIR Organization resource representing companies, institutions, departments, or healthcare practice groups. For direct read, provide _id alone. For search, provide identifier, name, or partof (parent organization).

searchParams._id: Organization resource ID — performs direct read when provided alone
searchParams.active: Whether the organization's record is active
searchParams.address-use: Use of the address (work, temp, old, billing)
searchParams.endpoint: Technical endpoints providing access to services
searchParams.name: A portion of the organization's name or alias
searchParams.phonetic: A portion of the name using phonetic matching
searchParams.type: A code for the type of organization

description: Search for or read a FHIR Patient resource. For direct read, provide _id alone. For search, you MUST supply one of these minimum data sets — name alone is never sufficient: (1) identifier (MRN, FHIR ID, or other system|value identifier), (2) given + family + birthdate, or (3) given + family + legal-sex (or gender) + telecom. Always ask the user for enough information to satisfy one of these sets before searching.

searchParams._id: Patient resource ID — performs direct read when provided alone
searchParams.name: Any part of the patient's name — ignored when family or given is also provided
searchParams.birthdate: Date of birth in YYYY-MM-DD format
searchParams.gender: Administrative gender: female, male, other, unknown
searchParams.legal-sex: Legal sex — takes precedence over gender when both are provided: female, male, nonbinary, x, unknown
searchParams.telecom: Phone number or email address (prefix # to skip phone validation, + for international)
searchParams.identifier: Business identifier in system|value format (e.g. MRN, SSN, FHIR ID)

description: Search for or read a FHIR Practitioner resource. For direct read, provide _id alone. For search, provide at least one of: identifier, name, given + family, email, phone, or telecom. This resource represents healthcare providers — physicians, nurses, pharmacists, etc.

searchParams._id: Practitioner resource ID — performs direct read when provided alone
searchParams.address-use: Use of the address (home, work, temp, old, billing)
searchParams.name: Any part of the practitioner's name
searchParams.phonetic: A portion of name using phonetic matching

description: Search for or read a FHIR PractitionerRole resource linking practitioners to organizations, locations, specialties, and services. For direct read, provide _id alone. For search, provide at least one scoping param such as practitioner, organization, location, role, specialty, or identifier.

searchParams._id: PractitionerRole resource ID — performs direct read when provided alone
searchParams.active: Whether this practitioner role record is active
searchParams.date: The period during which the practitioner is authorized for these roles
searchParams.endpoint: Technical endpoints providing access to services
searchParams.location: Location where this practitioner provides care
searchParams.service: Healthcare services provided for this role

description: Search for or read a FHIR Procedure resource for surgical, diagnostic, and therapeutic procedures. For direct read, provide _id alone. For search, provide patient, subject, identifier, encounter, based-on, or part-of. Filter by code (SNOMED CT), date, or status.

searchParams._id: Procedure resource ID — performs direct read when provided alone
searchParams.based-on: Reference to the CarePlan or ServiceRequest that authorized the procedure
searchParams.date: Date or date range the procedure was performed
searchParams.encounter: Encounter during which the procedure was performed
searchParams.location: Where the procedure was performed (Location reference)
searchParams.part-of: Part of referenced event (Procedure, Observation, MedicationAdministration)
searchParams.reason-code: Coded reason the procedure was performed
searchParams.reason-reference: Condition or Observation that is the reason
searchParams.status: Status: preparation, in-progress, not-done, on-hold, stopped, completed, entered-in-error, unknown
searchParams.subject: Subject of the procedure (Patient or Group)

description: Search for or read a FHIR ServiceRequest resource (orders and referrals for procedures, diagnostics, therapies). For direct read, provide _id alone. For search, provide patient, subject, identifier, encounter, requester, based-on, or requisition. Use code to identify the service ordered and intent/status to filter.

searchParams._id: ServiceRequest resource ID — performs direct read when provided alone
searchParams.based-on: What request fulfills (CarePlan, MedicationRequest, or ServiceRequest reference)
searchParams.body-site: Where procedure is going to be done (coded body site)
searchParams.code: What is being requested/ordered (SNOMED CT, LOINC)
searchParams.encounter: Encounter in which this request was made
searchParams.intent: Intent: proposal, plan, directive, order, original-order, reflex-order, filler-order, instance-order, option
searchParams.status: Status: draft, active, on-hold, revoked, completed, entered-in-error, unknown
searchParams.subject: Subject of the request (Patient, Group, Location, or Device)
